  Extracted: 6. **Works really well, but its cable is not very good.** by **Anthony SanchezAnthony Sanchez** (Posted on 4/21/23) - Rating: 80%
  Extracted:    I have been using this as my main N64 controller for about a month now, and I am satisfied with its build quality. The analog stick is far superior over the original, since it feels slightly softer and it resets to the neutral position 100% of the time; the buttons and D-pad feel just right; and the expansion port works perfectly fine. However, while the cable is nice and long, it tangles up too easily, and despite taking good care of it, there are already several tears all throughout the cable, exposing the wire on the inside. They just...appeared, from normal use, most likely because the cable material is weak despite being so bendsome. So while the controller itself is great, you should probably ready some tape to protect the wiring of the cable.
  Extracted: 
  Extracted: 7. **It's okay...** by **cjgirl864** (Posted on 4/21/23) - Rating: 60%
  Extracted:    I was initially very happy with this controller as it seemed sturdy enough without the joystick being too stiff. If you've ever been an avid N64 player you know that the joysticks on these controllers never hold up over time. This one starter good but after a few weeks it seems to wiggle out of the neutral position and move my character without my help. Usually in some spastic way that results in Mario falling off a cliff. It isn't so bad right now but I suspect it'll need to be replaced again soon. I will keep trying different brands but honestly I don't think it's the brand or construction of this controller. I think the design of the N64 controllers just lends itself to breaking or wearing out easily. It's a shame because some of my favorite games are on the 64. But because of that I'll just keep repurchasing controllers as needed. At least the price is good and this one does seem really close to the original Nintendo controller so it feels natural and comfortable in my hands.
